content:
First, label the trapezoid so that \(BC\) and \(AD\) are the two bases with \(BC>AD\), and the legs are \(AB=CD=\ell\).  Set
\[
BC=b,\quad AD=a,\quad AB=CD=\ell,
\]
and let the height be \(h\).  From the given perimeters
\[
a+b+2\ell=120\quad\text{(trapezoid)}, 
\qquad b+\ell+AC=114\quad\text{(triangle }ABC),
\]
we get
\[
AC=d=114-b-\ell,
\qquad a=120-b-2\ell.
\]
By placing \(B=(0,0)\), \(C=(b,0)\), \(A\) and \(D\) symmetrically at height \(h\), one finds
\[
\ell^2=\Bigl(\tfrac{b-a}2\Bigr)^2+h^2,
\qquad
d^2=\Bigl(\tfrac{b+a}2\Bigr)^2+h^2.
\]
Subtracting gives
\[
d^2-\ell^2=ab.
\]
On the other hand the distance of the incenters of \(\triangle ABC\) and \(\triangle DBC\) is horizontal and is given by
\[
\frac{b\,(a+d-\ell)}{114}=16
\;\;\Longrightarrow\;\;
b\,(a+d-\ell)=1824.
\]
Substituting \(d=114-b-\ell\) and \(a=120-b-2\ell\) into these two relations and solving one finds
\[
b=48,\quad \ell=25,\quad a=22,\quad d=41,
\]
and then
\[
h^2=\ell^2-\Bigl(\tfrac{b-a}2\Bigr)^2
=25^2-13^2=625-169=456,
\quad
h=\sqrt{456}=2\sqrt{114}.
\]
Finally the area of the trapezoid is
\[
\Area \;=\;\frac{(b+a)}2\;h
\;=\;\frac{48+22}2\;\cdot2\sqrt{114}
\;=\;35\cdot2\sqrt{114}
\;=\;70\sqrt{114}.
\]
Hence \(m=70\), \(n=114\), and
\[
100m+n=7000+114=7114.
\]
\boxed{7114}

BC=b,\quad AD=a,\quad AB=CD=\ell,
\]
and let the height be \(h\).  From the given perimeters
\[
a+b+2\ell=120\quad\text{(trapezoid)}, 
\qquad b+\ell+AC=114\quad\text{(triangle }ABC),
\]
we get
\[
AC=d=114-b-\ell,
\qquad a=120-b-2\ell.
\]
By placing \(B=(0,0)\), \(C=(b,0)\), \(A\) and \(D\) symmetrically at height \(h\), one finds
\[
\ell^2=\Bigl(\tfrac{b-a}2\Bigr)^2+h^2,
\qquad
d^2=\Bigl(\tfrac{b+a}2\Bigr)^2+h^2.
\]
Subtracting gives
\[
d^2-\ell^2=ab.
\]
On the other hand the distance of the incenters of \(\triangle ABC\) and \(\triangle DBC\) is horizontal and is given by
\[
\frac{b\,(a+d-\ell)}{114}=16
\;\;\Longrightarrow\;\;
b\,(a+d-\ell)=1824.
\]
Substituting \(d=114-b-\ell\) and \(a=120-b-2\ell\) into these two relations and solving one finds
\[
b=48,\quad \ell=25,\quad a=22,\quad d=41,
\]
and then
\[
h^2=\ell^2-\Bigl(\tfrac{b-a}2\Bigr)^2
=25^2-13^2=625-169=456,
\quad

\[
\Area \;=\;\frac{(b+a)}2\;h

\;=\;35\cdot2\sqrt{114}
\;=\;70\sqrt{114}.
\]

\[
100m+n=7000+114=7114.
\]
\boxed{7114}
